--cd:
--cd <Directory>
Start in the specified Windows-based directory
if using ~, it will use the home directory of the Linux user
If the path starts with a / character, it will be interpreted as an absolute Linux path
Otherwise, the value must be an absolute Windows path
--shell-type:
✨--shell-type <Type>
Execute the specified command using the provided shell type
--shell-type standard: Execute the specified command using the default Linux shell.
--shell-type login: Execute the specified command using the default Linux shell as the login shell
--shell-type none: Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell
--debug-shell: Open a debug shell for WSL2 diagnostics
--event-viewer: Open the Application view of Windows Event Viewer
--install:
✨--install [Distro] [Options...]
Install a Linux Windows Subsystem distribution.
To see the available distribution list, use 'wsl --list --online'
--install --web-download: Download the distribution from the internet instead of Microsoft Store
--mount:
✨--mount <Disk>
Attach and mount a physical or virtual disk in all WSL 2 distributions
--mount --vhd: Specify that <Disk> represents a virtual hard disk
--mount --bare: Attach the disk to WSL 2 but do not mount it
--mount --name:
--name <Name>
Use a custom name for the mount point
--mount --type:
--type <Type>
Filesystem to use when mounting the disk; defaults to ext4 if not specified.
--mount --partition:
--partition <Index>
Index of the partition to mount; defaults to the entire disk if not specified
--release-notes: Open a web browser to view the WSL release notes page
--shutdown: Terminate all running distributions and the WSL 2 lightweight virtual machine
--status: Display the status of Windows Subsystem for Linux
--unmount:
--unmount [Disk]
Unmount and detach a disk from all WSL 2 distributions.
If called without arguments, unmount and detach all disks
--update --web-download: Download updates from the Internet instead of Microsoft Store.
--export:
✨--export <Distro> <FileName> [Options]
Export a distribution as a tar file. For standard output, the filename can be "-"(dash)
e.g. wsl --export ubuntu "$env:UserProfile\Desktop\export.tar"
--export --vhd: Specify that the distribution is to be exported as a .vhdx file
--import:
✨--import <Distro> <InstallLocation> <FileName> [Options]
Import a specified tar as a new distribution. For standard input, the filename can be "-"(dash)
e.g. wsl --import aaa "$env:UserProfile\Desktop" "$env:UserProfile\Desktop\export.tar"
--import --version:
--version <Version>
Specify the version to use for the new distribution
--import --vhd:
--version <Version>
Specify that the provided file is a .vhdx file rather than a tar file.
This will create a copy of the .vhdx file at the specified installation location
--import-in-place:
--import-in-place <Distro> <FileName>
Import a specified .vhdx as a new distribution. This virtual hard disk must be formatted with an ext4 filesystem
--set-version:
--set-version <Distro> <Version>
Change the version of the specified distribution
--set-default-version:
--set-default-version <Version>
Change the default installation version for new distributions.
--unregister:
{{if($Distro_list){'✨'} }}--unregister <Distro>
Unregister and remove the root filesystem for the specified distribution

wsl_core_info.Distro-u: Unregister and remove the root filesystem for the distribution -- {{$Distro}}
wsl_core_info.-u:
--user, -u <UserName>
Run as the specified Linux user
wsl_core_info.-e:
--exec, -e <CommandLine>
Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell.
wsl_core_info.--system: Start a shell for the system distribution
wsl_core_info.-l-all: List all distributions, including those currently being installed or uninstalled
wsl_core_info.-l-v:
--verbose,-v
Show detailed information for all distributions
wsl_core_info.-l-o:
--online, -o
Show the available distribution list for installation
wsl_core_info.-s:
--set-default, -s <Distro>
Set the specified distribution as the default distribution
wsl_core_info.-v: Check version information for WSL and its components
